title: Pyrus thuringiaca
intro: Pyrus thuringiaca, commonly known as the wild pear or European mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Rosaceae. The health benefits of Pyrus thuringiaca include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus thuringiaca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus thuringiaca include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Pyrus thuringiaca is classified within the family Rosaceae. This family includes a wide range of flowering plants commonly known as rose family members.
